{{基礎情報 公家
| 氏名 = Usatsuhiko-no-Mikoto
| 画像 =
| 画像サイズ =
| 画像説明 =
| 時代 = {{ill|Antiquity|ja|古代|lt=Antiquity|lt_ja=上古}}, {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}
| 生誕 = Unknown
| 死没 = Unknown
| 改名 =
| 別名 = Usatsuhiko, Usatsuhiko-no-Mikoto
| 諡号 =
| 神号 =
| 戒名 =
| 墓所 =
| 官位 = First {{ill|Usa no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造}}
| 主君 = {{ill|Emperor Jinmu|ja|神武天皇}}
| 氏族 = {{ill|Usa clan|ja|宇佐氏}}
| 父母 = {{ill|Ame-no-Mikudari-no-Mikoto|ja|天三降命}}
| 兄弟 = {{ill|Usatsu-hime|ja|菟狭津媛|lt=Usatsu-hime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=宇佐津媛命}}
| 妻   =
| 子   = Tsunetsu-hiko-no-Mikoto
| 特記事項 =
}}
'''Usatsuhiko-no-Mikoto''' (''usatsuhiko-no-mikoto''; {{ill|dates of birth and death unknown|ja|生没年不詳|lt=dates of birth and death unknown|lt_ja=生没年不詳}}) was a {{ill|gōzoku|ja|豪族}} of ancient {{ill|Japan|ja|日本}} and the first '''{{ill|Usa no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造}}'''.

== Overview ==
In the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' he is written as '''Usatsuhiko'''; in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' and the "{{ill|Kōson Hongi|ja|皇孫本紀}}" as '''Usatsuhiko'''; in the "Kokuzō Hongi" as '''Usatsuhiko-no-Mikoto'''.

In the ''Kojiki'' he is identified as a figure of {{ill|Usa District|ja|宇佐郡|lt=Usa|lt_ja=宇沙}} in {{ill|Toyo Province|ja|豊国}}, and in the ''Nihon Shoki'' as a figure of {{ill|Usa|ja|宇佐郡|lt=Usa|lt_ja=菟狭}} in {{ill|Chikushi Province|ja|筑紫国}}; he appears together with his younger sister Usatsu-hime-no-Mikoto.

In the ''Nihon Shoki'', when {{ill|Emperor Jinmu|ja|神武天皇}} made his {{ill|Jinmu eastern expedition|ja|神武東征|lt=eastern expedition|lt_ja=東征}} and landed at {{ill|Usa|ja|宇佐郡|lt=Usa|lt_ja=菟狭}} in {{ill|Chikushi Province|ja|筑紫国}}, with {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko|ja|椎根津彦|lt=Uzuhiko|lt_ja=珍彦}}, the {{ill|kunitsukami|ja|国津神|lt=Kunitsukami|lt_ja=国神}}, as sea guide, bound for the Nakatsukuni ({{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}), Usatsuhiko and {{ill|Usatsu-hime|ja|菟狭津媛}} — the ancestors of the {{ill|Usa no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|lt=Usa no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt_ja=菟狭国造}} — were there; they built a one-pillared raised palace (''ashi-hitotsu agari no miya'') at the upper reaches of the {{ill|Usa River|ja|駅館川|lt=Usa River|lt_ja=菟狭川}} and gave a banquet in service. There Emperor Jinmu issued a decree and had the younger sister Usatsu-hime married to the attendant minister {{ill|Amanotane-ko-no-Mikoto|ja|天種子命}} ({{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏|lt=Nakatomi-no-Muraji|lt_ja=中臣連}} distant ancestor). The ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' and the ''Kojiki'' record similar traditions, but the ''Kojiki'' has no description of Amanotane-ko-no-Mikoto.

{{quote|Usa District
Usatsuhiko-no-Mikoto (loyalty to the ruler) (reverence for the gods) (just cause)

He was the son of {{ill|Ame-no-Mikudari-no-Mikoto|ja|天三降命|lt=Ame-no-Mikudari-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=天御降命}} — grandson of {{ill|Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊|lt=Takamimusu-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=高皇産令命}} — and resided at Usa-jima. He served Hime-Ōkami, and when Emperor Jinmu reached Usa during his eastern expedition and lodged there for a time, he built the one-pillared raised palace and offered him hospitality. He is the ancestor of the {{ill|Usa clan|ja|宇佐氏}}, and his descendants have branched into several hundred families including the {{ill|Miyanari clan|ja|宮成氏|lt=Miyanari|lt_ja=宮成}} and the {{ill|Itōzu clan|ja|到津氏|lt=Itōzu|lt_ja=到津}} {{ill|baron|ja|男爵}} houses.|''Shūshinka Kyōdo Shiryō Shūsei''{{sfn|大分県教育会|1936}}}}

== Genealogy ==
{{see |{{ill|Usa clan|ja|宇佐氏}}}}
He is said to have been the son of {{ill|Ame-no-Mikudari-no-Mikoto|ja|天三降命}} (son of {{ill|Ame-no-Ikutama-no-Mikoto|ja|天活玉命}}), grandson of {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊}} (''takamimusubi'').

His younger sister Usatsu-hime-no-Mikoto became the wife of {{ill|Amanotane-ko-no-Mikoto|ja|天種子命}} and bore {{ill|Usatsu-omi-no-Mikoto|ja|宇佐津臣命}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏|lt=Nakatomi-no-Muraji|lt_ja=中臣連}}.

== Footnotes ==
{{脚注ヘルプ}}
=== Notes ===
{{notelist}}
=== Citations ===
{{Reflist|2}}

== References ==
*{{anchors|大分県教育会}}{{Cite book|和書|author=大分県教育会| translator=| title=[{{NDLDC|918478/45}} 修身科郷土資料集成]| page=73| publisher=大分県教育会| location =大分県| year=1936| isbn=| ref=harv}}

== See also ==
* {{ill|List of Figures from before the Asuka Period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}}
* {{ill|Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}}

{{神道 横}}
{{日本神話}}
{{Normdaten}}
{{デフォルトソート:うさつひこのみこと}}
[[Category:宇佐氏|*うさつひこのみこと]]
[[Category:生没年不詳]]
[[category:古代日本の人物]]
[[Category:豊国の人物]]
[[Category:豊前国の人物]]
[[Category:大分県の歴史]]
[[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]]
[[ja:菟狭津彦命]]
